Title: A peroxisomal b-oxidative pathway contributes to the formation of C6–C1 aromatic volatiles in poplar

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: C6-C1 benzenoid chain-shortening from cinnamic acid to benzoyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool; activates cinnamic acid for conversion to benzoyl-CoA and downstream volatile benzenoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates cinnamic acid derivatives to the corresponding CoA esters in an ATP-dependent reaction
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as key control step together with PtCNL4; strongest herbivory-induced CNL and RNAi of CNL1/4 reduced benzenoid volatile emission
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes herbivore-induced emission of benzaldehyde, benzylalcohol, benzyl benzoate, and methyl salicylate via volatile benzenoid formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in E. coli; purified enzyme assay; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; RNAi knockdown in Populus x canescens; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtCNL1 encodes an active cinnamate-CoA ligase preferring trans-cinnamic acid and hydroxycinnamic acids. 2. Its expression is strongly induced by Chrysomela populi herbivory. 3. Joint RNAi knockdown with CNL4 decreases herbivore-induced volatile benzenoids but not salicinoids, supporting a role in volatile benzenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Barely expressed in undamaged leaves; strongly induced in beetle-damaged leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in RNAi lines in herbivore-damaged leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: benzaldehyde; benzylalcohol; benzyl benzoate; methyl salicylate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense against herbivores; aromatic defense volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AMP-binding acyl-activating enzyme family; acyl-CoA synthetase-like
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi reduced volatile benzenoid emission but did not alter salicinoids; no broader major effects on most terpenoids reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate herbivore-induced benzenoid volatile defense in poplar; stacking with downstream pathway genes may enhance volatile output
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PtCNL1 and PtCNL2 were identical; PtCNL3 was not detectably expressed in leaves and could not be cloned

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: C6-C1 benzenoid chain-shortening from cinnamic acid to benzoyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool; activates cinnamic acid for conversion to benzoyl-CoA and downstream volatile benzenoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates cinnamic acid derivatives to their CoA esters in an ATP-dependent reaction
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as key control step together with PtCNL1; strongest herbivory-induced CNL and target of effective RNAi
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes herbivore-induced emission of volatile benzenoids including benzaldehyde, benzylalcohol, benzyl benzoate, and methyl salicylate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in E. coli; purified enzyme assay; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; RNAi knockdown in Populus x canescens; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtCNL4 is a highly active cinnamate-CoA ligase using cinnamic acid and hydroxycinnamic acids. 2. It is strongly induced by herbivory in P. trichocarpa leaves. 3. Knockdown together with CNL1 lowers benzenoid volatile emission without affecting salicinoids, indicating involvement in volatile benzenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Barely expressed in undamaged leaves; strongly induced in beetle-damaged leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in RNAi lines in herbivore-damaged leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: benzaldehyde; benzylalcohol; benzyl benzoate; methyl salicylate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense against herbivores; aromatic defense volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AMP-binding acyl-activating enzyme family; acyl-CoA synthetase-like
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockdown reduced volatile benzenoids but not salicinoids; little effect on most other herbivore-induced volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to enhance or suppress induced benzenoid volatile defenses in poplar
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Figure 3 specifically demonstrates PtCNL4 enzyme activity

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: C6-C1 benzenoid chain-shortening from cinnamoyl-CoA to 3-oxo intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrates and dehydrogenates cinnamoyl-CoA derivatives to the corresponding 3-oxo acyl-CoA intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports conversion of activated cinnamic acid derivatives toward benzoyl-CoA in volatile benzenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in E. coli; purified enzyme assay; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtCHD1 is a bifunctional hydratase/dehydrogenase active with cinnamoyl-CoA derivatives. 2. It showed the highest in vitro activity among PtCHD candidates. 3. Its expression is modestly induced by herbivory, consistent with participation in the β-oxidative benzenoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in leaves and significantly upregulated by herbivory
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bifunctional hydratase/dehydrogenase with conserved hydratase and dehydrogenase motifs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate supportive engineering target for reconstructing benzoyl-CoA formation in poplar or heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used in coupled assays to generate KAT substrates

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: C6-C1 benzenoid chain-shortening terminal thiolytic step to benzoyl-CoA
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms benzoyl-CoA, the C6-C1 benzenoid building block
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes thiolytic cleavage of 3-oxo phenylpropanoyl-CoA intermediates to shorten the side chain by two carbons, yielding benzoyl-CoA derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates benzoyl-CoA precursor for volatile benzenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression in E. coli; coupled enzyme assay with PtCHD1; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtKAT1 efficiently catalyzes thiolytic shortening of 3-oxo aromatic acyl-CoAs in vitro. 2. It is significantly induced by herbivory. 3. The data support its role in forming benzoyl-CoA for benzenoid volatile production.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in leaves and significantly induced by herbivory
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: thiolase family with conserved thiolase motifs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream target for engineering benzoyl-CoA supply to benzenoid volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PtKAT2 also showed in vitro activity but was not herbivory-induced and was not directly functionally perturbed in planta
